prompt: You draw 5 cards at random from a standard 52-card deck. The odds of getting a royal flush (10, J, Q, K, A of the same suit) are 1 in N. What is N?
source: Standard 5-card draw combinatorics. C(52,5) = 2,598,960 possible hands; 4 are royal flushes (one per suit). 2,598,960 / 4 = 649,740.
revealHeadline: Four hands out of two-and-a-half million.
revealExplanation: There are C(52, 5) = 2,598,960 distinct 5-card hands, and only 4 are royal flushes (one per suit). The probability is 4 / 2,598,960 = 1 / 649,740. Most people sense this is rare but underestimate by 1–2 orders of magnitude.
